The '''Imperial repeater rifle''', also known as the '''Imperial repeater gun''' or '''Autogun''', was a type of [[Repeating blaster/Legends|repeating blaster]] prevalent during the time of and immediately after the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==History==
[[File:Imperial_Dark_Trooper.jpg|thumb|left|Dark Troopers used repeater rifles|200px]]
A large version the weapon was originally developed by [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] [[Rebus (Galactic Empire)|Rebus]] in the [[Weapons Research Facility]] of [[Fest/Legends|Fest]], as a prototype weapon for the [[Dark Trooper/Legends|Dark troopers]]; it was first used it in the [[Attack on Tak Base]] of [[Talay/Legends|Talay]]. One such prototype, said to be too large to be carried by a regular trooper, was [[Mission to Talay|discovered]] by [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Agent/Legends|agent]] [[Kyle Katarn]]; the insignia on the weapon led the Rebellion to Rebus. Later seen Dark Troopers however used the [[Assault cannon/Legends|assault cannon]].<ref name="DF">''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces]]''</ref>

The weapon was primarily found in [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] armories, as the Fest facility, where Katarn found a useable version of; around that time it was also found in [[Space station/Legends|space stations]] including [[Fuel Station Ergo/Legends|Fuel Station Ergo]].<ref name="DFG">''[[Dark Forces Official Player's Guide]]''</ref>

After the end of the Galactic Civil War in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], a more compact versıon of the weapon was adopted by the [[Imperial Remnant]] forces under the leadership of the [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] [[Jerec (Inquisitor)|Jerec]]. It continued in the service of the remnant through [[10 ABY/Legends|10 ABY]] during [[Operation Shadow Hand]]. By [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]] Remnant forces adopted to the [[Imperial Heavy Repeater]], replacing the old weapon entirely.

==Description==
A fast and highly accurate ranged weapon, the Imperial repeater rifle featured two firing modes: a single shot mode and a slower tri-shot burst mode that fired in a triangular configuration. The tri-shot mode was powerful against [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], as they could only block one or two bolts with their lightsaber, while one would always get through. The primary firing mode featured a very high rate of fire that could cut through swaths of enemies in little time, although this brought about the weapon's main flaw; Due to the high rate of fire, the Repeater could burn through ammunition extremely quickly. The primary mode's pinpoint accuracy, however, more than made up for the ammo consumption, as the weapon could effectively be used at great distances, although the projectiles lost momentum the longer they went, thus long range hits wouldn't be as damaging. Granted, the weapon wasn't as damaging as blasters anyway due to the lightness of the projectiles and the lack of force behind the bullets.

The weapon's housing was constructed in a fairly angular fashion, the barrel housing of the weapon constructed like a triangle. There were three barrels overall, one in each corner of the triangular housing. This was the feature that enabled the tri-shot mode. A drum was attached to the right side of the repeater's body which housed the ammunition. The internal belt was wrapped around the insides of the weapon, and the belt and the rounds were be visible on the left side of the weapon. The projectiles did not use casings. The grip and trigger was on the back of the main body. Most models were equipped with a wood grip. A long thin stock protruded from the back of the grip at the bottom.

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:ImperialRepeaterRifle.png|thumb|First-person view of the repeater rifle|200px]]
The rifle first appeared in the video game ''[[Star Wars: Dark Forces]]'' as one of the weapons that the player can equip and use. During development of the game, the Auto Gun was one of the universally strongest weapons that outperformed all others; its power was rationalised in the finished game in order to encourage more diverse strategy from the player.<ref name="DFG" />

According to [[Justin Chin]], the [[Phase II Dark Trooper/Legends|Phase II]] [[Dark Trooper/Legends|dark trooper]] was originally going to carry a Imperial Repeater with a ribbon chain clip to an ammo pack slung in the back. However, he doesn't make clear if 'originally' refers to the game designers' or the Imperials' intention.

The 'Dark Trooper Weapon' Katarn finds in the [[Mission to Talay|second level]] of the game matches indeed a repeater rifle in appearance. Katarn observes that the weapon is too large to have been carried by a normal human soldier, and is not equippable. However the Repeater appears again in a later level, perfectly usable by Kyle and anyone else and is very much prevalent (and actually appears quite compact) in the sequel, [[Star Wars: Jedi Knight: Dark Forces II|''Jedi Knight'']].

The resource files for ''Dark Forces'' contain an animation labeled "IDTGUN", corresponding with a single IDTGUN sprite which is the prototype weapon the player recovers. However, the DTGUN animation is a placeholder animation of a simple character jumping.

The ''[[Dark Forces Official Player's Guide]]'' provides 'Auto Gun' as the gun's "nickname", without making clear if it was also used in-universe.
